reasoning: Indirect nuclear talks between the U.S. and Iran resumed in April 2025 but hit a stalemate in early June over the core issue of domestic uranium enrichment. President Trump imposed a 60-day deadline for a deal which has since expired. Following reported Israeli attacks earlier in June, Iran canceled the planned sixth round of negotiations and ramped up its nuclear activities. Subsequently, reports from June 21-22 indicate the U.S. militarily struck three Iranian nuclear sites, initiating a major regional conflict. Given this severe kinetic escalation—involving active U.S. and Israeli airstrikes as well as Iranian drone retaliations—the diplomatic process is completely derailed. While coercive diplomacy is theoretically possible, reaching and signing a complex nuclear agreement within the remaining two-month window (by September 1, 2025) is now extraordinarily unlikely.
